title: The Six Generations of Code
overview: A classification system used in the Atlas to group languages by their chronological and hardware-driven context.
historical_context: Derived from the traditional 1GL-5GL taxonomy, but refined for the systems-focused Narrative of the Atlas.
mental_model: The evolution of the "Atoms" of computing across six distinct eras.
tradeoffs: Generations are often fluid and overlap; a language born in "cloud" may still have "early" design principles.
legacy: Provides the structural backbone for the Atlas timeline and dashboards.
ai_assisted_discovery_missions: Trace the transition from "dawn" to "early" via the invention of the compiler.
Analyze why the "cloud" era favored memory safety over absolute manual control.
Predict the core characteristics of the next generation beyond "autonomic".
